description:
**Job Description**
 Step into the heart of Asia’s dynamic markets as an Analyst on our Asia Macro Strategy team. In this junior research role, you’ll be instrumental in driving our research agenda and delivering impactful insights to clients. If you’re passionate about macroeconomics, financial markets, and the fast\-evolving APAC region, this is an exciting opportunity to launch your career and develop specialized expertise. With APAC as a major growth engine for our business, we’re looking for a high\-caliber candidate eager to make a difference.
   

  

 As a Macro Strategy Analyst in Wealth Management, you’ll collaborate with a global network of Strategists, Economists, and Asset Class Specialists. Your responsibilities will include performing in\-depth analysis, conducting research, and crafting reports that articulate J.P. Morgan Private Bank’s investment perspectives. You’ll support senior Strategists in shaping core macroeconomic views, generating investment ideas, and developing asset allocation strategies. Your day\-to\-day will involve data analysis, content publication, and trade idea generation, with a clear path to building specialized expertise over time.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Conduct research and analysis on economic and financial market developments, supporting the generation of actionable investment recommendations.
* Assist in formulating macro and asset class views, with a focus on Asia macroeconomics as well as global interest rates and currencies.
* Contribute to the creation of publications and market commentaries for clients, the media, and internal stakeholders, helping to communicate J.P. Morgan Private Bank’s perspectives and support informed investment decisions.
* Present economic and market analysis, along with investment recommendations, to both internal teams and external clients.
* Collaborate with senior team members on ad\-hoc research projects and contribute to the development of proprietary models and analytical tools.
* Stay up to date on key macroeconomic data releases, policy developments, and market\-moving events.


**Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills**
* Bachelor’s degree in Economics or related disciplines
* Dynamic team player who is self\-motivated and possesses strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to proactively engage multiple stakeholders at all levels of seniority to drive the research agenda and operate with a strong sense of urgency.
* Strong proficiency in both English and Chinese (reading and writing) is required.


**Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills**
* Advanced degrees preferred.
* Experience in the financial industry
* Research and/or investment advisory experience preferred.
* Understanding of the Wealth Management business is preferred.


**ABOUT US**
 J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.
   

  

 We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.
   

  

**About The Team**
 J.P. Morgan Asset \& Wealth Management delivers industry\-leading investment management and private banking solutions. Asset Management provides individuals, advisors and institutions with strategies and expertise that span the full spectrum of asset classes through our global network of investment professionals. Wealth Management helps individuals, families and foundations take a more intentional approach to their wealth or finances to better define, focus and realize their goals.

description:
**Job Description**
**About Goldman Sachs**
 Goldman Sachs Asset Management is one of the world’s leading asset managers with over $3 trillion in assets under supervision. As the primary investment area within Goldman Sachs, we provide investment and advisory services for pension plans, sovereign wealth funds, insurance companies, endowments, foundations, financial advisors, and individuals.
   

  

**External Investing Group (XIG)**
 XIG provides investors with investment solutions, across leading hedge fund managers, private equity funds, real estate managers, public equity strategies and fixed income strategies. XIG manages globally diversified programs, targeted sector\-specific strategies, customized portfolios, and a range of advisory services. Our investors access opportunities through new fund commitments, fund\-of\-fund investments, strategic partnerships, secondary\-market investments, co\-investments, and seed\-capital investments. With over 400 professionals across 11 offices around the world, XIG provides manager diligence, portfolio construction, risk management, and liquidity solutions to investors, drawing on Goldman Sachs’ market insights and risk management expertise. We extend these global capabilities to the world’s leading sovereign wealth funds, pension plans, governments, financial institutions, endowments, foundations, and family offices, for which we invest or advise on over $500 billion of alternative investments, public equity strategies and fixed income strategies.
   

  

**XIG Hedge Funds**
 The XIG hedge fund team is the part of the XIG responsible for sourcing, examining, and investing in leading external hedge fund managers. The team has been in this business since 1969, making it one of the first multi\-managers in the industry. As of December 2024, the team managed over $32 billion across portfolios of hedge funds, customized solutions, and advisory relationships. The team covers a wide number of strategies including fundamental equity long short, credit, event driven, macro, CTAs, multi\-strategy and quantitative funds. Investors include public pensions, corporate pensions, financial institutions, endowments, foundations, and high net worth individuals. This diverse and broad investor base is beneficial for XIG, both as a source of capital and as a resource for us in the markets where we invest. Our group has sought to establish deep and longstanding relationships with leading institutional hedge fund investors and have benefited from Goldman Sachs’ strong relationships with ultra\-high net worth individuals. Within hedge funds, our history in managing portfolios dates as far back as 1969, meaning we are among the most experienced hedge fund advisors in the industry.
   

  

**Alternatives Capital Formation (ACF)**
 ACF manages capital raising and strategy for alternative investments including private equity, private credit, real estate, infrastructure, growth equity, sustainability, secondaries, GP stakes, and hedge funds / liquid alternatives. Within ACF, team members work within product verticals and collaborate across strategies to identify portfolio\-wide solutions. They also partner with other teams within ACF, and across CSG, investing teams and wealth management teams. Professional development initiatives provide opportunities to enhance expertise across alternative investment products, and to sharpen market knowledge from industry experts investing across the Firm. This specific ACF role sits with XIG, focused on hedge funds.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
**RESPONSIBILITIES AND QUALIFICATIONS:**
* As a junior product specialist, individual would be responsible for helping senior team members drive commercial strategy, capital formation \& client engagement. This involves assisting in the launch, management of strategy, and ongoing business development for various funds/products across regions and client channels.
* Additional responsibilities include ability to:
+ Support content development for existing client and prospect presentations, internal governance, Request for Proposals, and other investment\-related analyses
+ Prepare regular quantitative and qualitative content market views and platform offerings
+ Support the XIG ACF team’s broader initiatives focused on enhancing client service, data efficiency, and infrastructure development


**Qualifications**
* 1\-3 years in a similar or related role
* Specific interest in capital formation and business development
* Strong interest in capital markets, asset management, and understanding of alternative investments
* Excellent analytical, presentation, communication and interpersonal skills
* Strong organizational skills to oversee production of a high volume of quality work within tight timeframes, often including multiple simultaneous projects
* Attention to detail – proven experience of delivering high levels of accuracy
* Knowledge and understanding of Excel, PowerPoint and Word and adaptability to other software products
* Strong communication skills with ability to convey ideas in an impactful manner
* Excellent command of English and Chinese (Mandarin and Cantonese is a strong plus)


**About Goldman Sachs**
 At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world.
   

  

 We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers.
   

  

 We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability\-statement.html

description:
****The Hong Kong Jockey Club****
 Founded in 1884, The Hong Kong Jockey Club (“the Club”) is a world\-class racing club that acts continuously for the betterment of our society. The Club has a unique integrated business model, comprising racing and racecourse entertainment, a membership club, responsible sports wagering and lottery, and charities and community contribution. Through this model, the Club generates economic and social value for the community and supports the HKSAR Government in combatting illegal gambling.
   

  

****The Division****
 The Racing Division is committed to managing all aspects of Hong Kong’s world\-class sport of horse racing, covering the full value chain — from importation and training to racedays, equestrian, welfare, integrity, and international engagement. With Conghua Racecourse and Greater Bay Area expansion, we’re shaping racing into a dynamic sporting, social, and entertainment experience.
   

  

****The Department****
 The Commingling Department plays an essential role in the development of Hong Kong as a world class racing jurisdiction and in disseminating Hong Kong racing betting product to a global audience. Through co\-operations with overseas racing betting operators and media, this team cultivates and develops business opportunities for incoming, outgoing simulcast and the commingling business including World Pool. This team is responsible for the conduct of business and strategic analysis, contract negotiation and maintenance as well as marketing activities in relation to commingling matters abroad.
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The job holder reports to Senior Manager, Business Development of the Commingling Development and is responsible for executing the business development strategy, with a core focus on driving growth of the commingling business, securing partnerships with overseas partners / operators, and expanding the international footprint of Hong Kong racing.
   

  

****The Job****
* Collaborate with stakeholders to align racing content and marketing materials with business strategy; Help develop customer incentive programs to lead financial upside
* Plan, create and publish digital content for the World Pool website, ensuring accuracy and visual consistency
* Liaise with agency to develop key visuals and series of web banners on monthly basis and deliver according to the posting schedule
* Distribute promotional content across various channels (social media, blog, email, offline) and drive marketing outcomes to enhance World Pool brand awareness and customer engagement
* Establish and maintain relationships with overseas partners and media agency to grow World Pool as an international brand
* Work with internal stakeholders like Finance, Procurement and Legal for the drafting, negotiation and execution of marketing agreements related to overseas racing and World Pool
* Manage marketing expenses and budgets, issue purchase orders and responsible for other marketing administrative tasks
* Ensures compliance with Responsible Gambling Policy (RGP) and all stipulated legal requirements pertaining to wagering, thereby protecting the integrity of the Club's business
* Undertakes other duties as assigned by Line Manager


**About You**
* Bachelor degree in Marketing, Business Development or related disciplines
* A minimum of 5 years working experience
* Proven track record in project management, marketing planning and content strategy
* Operational expertise in social media from planning, stakeholders management and to execution
* Good negotiation, interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to prioritise multiple tasks and attention to detail
* Customer centric and strong marketing insight
* An understanding of the racing industry or international sports is highly advantageous


****Apply Now!****

description:
**About Us** 




 Longbridge is an AI\-Powered online brokerage that serves global investors with optimal trading experiences through building worldwide trading infrastructure and networks.
 



 Established in March 2019, Longbridge holds 22 financial licenses and qualifications across the United States, Singapore, Hong Kong, New Zealand and other regions, having secured over US $150 million in strategic investments from major financial and investment institutions. Its subsidiary, Longbridge Securities (Hong Kong) Limited (CE No.: BPX066\), holds Type 1, 2, 4, and 9 licenses issued by the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC).
 



  





**Role Overview** 




 You will be responsible for the end\-to\-end research, selection, and management of global fund products (including Mutual Funds, Private Equity, and ETFs). You will build robust research frameworks to evaluate fund managers, investment strategies, and underlying assets to provide actionable investment recommendations. This role bridges the gap between complex market data and client\-centric wealth solutions.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* **Fund Research \& Strategy:** 
 Build and maintain a comprehensive global fund research system. Conduct deep\-dive due diligence on fund managers, investment philosophies, and risk\-return profiles to produce institutional\-grade research reports.
* **Portfolio Construction \& Product Curation:** 
 Select high\-quality funds and manage the firm’s product list. Align product offerings with the company’s wealth management strategy and diverse client risk profiles to optimize portfolio structures.
* **Sales Enablement \& Investor Education:** 
 Lead the creation of investment education (InvEd) content and marketing collateral. Partner with relationship managers to provide technical product support, conduct training sessions, and enhance both team and client conviction in our product suite.
* **Market Intelligence \& Risk Management:** 
 Monitor global market trends, regulatory changes, and product innovations. Continuously refine selection criteria and research methodologies to mitigate risks and ensure full compliance.
* **Lifecycle Management \& Operations:** 
 Coordinate with Risk, Compliance, and Operations teams to streamline the entire product lifecycle—from initial onboarding and shelf\-launch to ongoing post\-investment monitoring.
* **Client\-Centric Optimization:** 
 Act as the feedback loop between front\-line business teams and the product department. Refine the research service system based on client needs to improve product\-suitability and drive AUM growth.



  





  





**Qualifications \& Requirements** 



* Bachelor’s degree or higher in Finance, Economics, Statistics, or Investment. Candidates with international education or overseas work experience are highly preferred.
* 3 years relevant experience in fund research, product selection, or investment analysis. Prior experience in a securities brokerage, wealth management firm, or asset management house is a significant advantage.
* Deep understanding of global fund investment strategies and risk assessment methodologies.Proven track record of producing independent, high\-quality research.
* Exceptional logical reasoning, and a results\-driven mindset. You should be a collaborative team player with a sharp sensitivity to market movements.
* Familiarity with Hong Kong financial regulations (SFC) is required. Professional designations such as CFA, FRM, or HKSI licensing is a plus.

 Varadise Limited is undergoing a strategic transformation to solidify its position as a leader in AI\-driven digital solutions.  The CTO will be responsible for the definition and delivery of the overall technology strategy, including the implementation of Varadise's digital transformation programme, collaborating with Top Management to ensure Varadise has the requisite technology infrastructure and capabilities to support business goals. The CTO will lead all digital innovations, improve efficiency, and demonstrate strong technology and business acumen, ensuring the right talent and competencies are built across the function.
 



  





**Key Outputs** 



* Maintain and guide a high\-achieving technology team.
* Develop a digital and information technology strategy aligned with Varadise's business goals.

**2\) Leadership and People Management**

* Proven leadership experience in spearheading cloud\-based and mobile application development.
* Significant professional background in agile software development lifecycle and/or deploying ERPs, with expertise in API, Web Services, and integration solutions.
* Proficiency in modern programming languages/frameworks (e.g., Python, C\#, Java, Node.js).

 Interested and qualified applicants should submit their CV, copies of certificates, and application letter to hr.dept@varadise.cloud with the SUBJECT OF THE EMAIL indicating the position being applied for.

description:
**About BTSE**
 BTSE Group is a global leader in fintech and blockchain technology, anchored by three core business pillars: Exchange, Payments, and Infrastructure Development. Serving over 100 corporate clients worldwide, we provide white\-label exchange and payment solutions. Our offerings encompass everything from exchange infrastructure hosting and development to custody, wallets, payments, blockchain integration, trading, and more. We are looking for talented professionals in marketing, operations, customer support, and other departments. The roles offered may be on\-site, remote, or hybrid, in collaboration with our local partner.
   

  

**About The Opportunity**
 We are seeking a highly skilled Quant Developer to join our trading system development team. You will play a critical role in building, optimising, and maintaining a high\-performance, low\-latency trading system. This is an exciting opportunity to work in a fast\-paced, collaborative environment and make a direct impact on trading strategies and operations.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Design, research, and validate systematic alpha factors across price, order book, funding, flow, and microstructure data
* Build and maintain a structured alpha research pipeline (data → feature → signal → evaluation → iteration)
* Conduct factor analysis including IC, IR, decay, stability, regime sensitivity, and turnover analysis
* Collaborate with engineering teams to ensure research outputs are production\-ready
* Continuously iterate and improve existing alpha signals, even if historical performance has decayed
* Explore AI\-assisted research workflows for factor generation, feature selection, and hypothesis exploration (bonus)


**Requirements**
* 3\+ years of quantitative research experience in systematic trading, alpha research, or related fields
* Strong proficiency in Python, with hands\-on experience using Jupyter Notebook as a primary research environment
* Solid understanding of the end\-to\-end alpha research process, including: Data cleaning \& normalization, Feature engineering, Factor construction, Signal evaluation \& validation.
* Have built and operated a complete alpha research framework (personal or professional)
* Proven experience discovering alpha factors with strong historical predictive power, e.g.: 1\. Information Coefficient (IC) consistently above 0\.05 –0\.1 on daily frequency or higher IC on lower\-frequency signals with reasonable stability (factors that later decayed are acceptable, as long as the original research process was sound)
* Strong analytical thinking and ability to explain why a factor works, not just that it works


**Nice to have**
* Experience using AI / ML models (e.g. tree models, neural networks, representation learning) for alpha research
* Hands\-on experience with local deployment of AI models (not just calling APIs)
* Familiarity with AI\-assisted factor discovery workflows (feature generation, signal screening, regime detection, etc.)
* Background in crypto, derivatives, or high\-frequency / microstructure\-driven markets

 With headquarters in Singapore and main operations across Asia, Europe, the Middle East and Latin America, Ant International is a leading global digital payment, digitisation and financial technology provider. Through collaboration across the private and public sectors, our unified techfin platform supports financial institutions and merchants of all sizes to achieve inclusive growth through a comprehensive range of cutting\-edge digital payment and financial services solutions. To learn more, please visit https://www.antglobal.com/
 



  





**ABOUT THE ROLE** 




 As a Product Intern at AlipayHK, you will have a unique opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in the fast\-paced world of fintech and digital payments. You will work alongside our product team to support the development and enhancement of one of Hong Kong's leading mobile payment platforms.
 



  





**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** 



* **Support Product Development:** 
 Assist in the product planning and development lifecycle for the AlipayHK App, contributing to initiatives that improve the overall user experience and journey.
* **Project Assistance:** 
 Help with the planning and execution of digital services products, including research on user needs, competitor analysis, and supporting commercialization initiatives.
* **Cross\-Functional Collaboration:** 
 Work closely with and learn from various teams, including Hong Kong business and operations teams, to support the implementation of product features that drive business growth.
* **Data \& User Research:** 
 Participate in user research, data analysis, and market studies to gather insights that inform product decisions.
* **Documentation \& Communication:** 
 Help create product requirement documents (PRDs), user stories, and presentations to communicate ideas and project statuses effectively.



  





**JOB REQUIREMENTS** 



* **Currently pursuing** 
 a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Marketing, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
* **Keen interest in product management:** 
 A strong passion for technology, user\-centric design, and creating digital products. Previous project or coursework related to product development is a significant plus.
* **Excellent communication skills:** 
 Strong written and verbal communication skills in both English and Mandarin.
* **Team player:** 
 Ability to collaborate effectively in a cross\-functional, dynamic environment. Proactive, eager to learn, and not afraid to ask questions.
* Available in
 **July to August 2026** 
 for
 **8 weeks** 
 .

description:
At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.
   

  

 In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.
   

  

 For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.
   

  

**A few examples of your responsibilities** 
* **Frame the right questions.** 
 Partner with product and growth manager to identify the most impactful questions—whether about pricing, feature adoption, or user behavior—and define how to answer them
* **Own product analytics and experiments.** 
 Design, implement, and analyze experiments end to end (e.g., deciding whether a feature should be paid or free), balancing short\-term impact with long\-term product health.
* **Translate data into insights.** 
 Provide timely, high\-quality insights to support prioritization, business planning, and product trade\-offs.
* **Make insights actionable.** 
 Translate analyses and model outputs into recommendations, using dashboards and lightweight reporting to help teams move quickly.
* **Leverage AI to accelerate work and uncover insights.** 
 Use AI tools to move faster, surface ideas and opportunities, and study competing products and trends.


**What we look for** 
* **Reasoning ability.** 
 Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter.
* **Drive.** 
 You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar.
* **Team spirit.** 
 You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent.
* **Proficiency in English.** 
 You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.


**What we offer** 
* **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** 
 You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field.
* **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** 
 We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too.
* **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** 
 Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.
* **All. These. Benefits.** 
 Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.


**Commitment \& contract**
 Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time or part\-time.
   

  

**Location**
 Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.
   

  

**The selection process**
 In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.
   

  

 If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.
   

  

 We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.
   

  

 We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.
   

  

**Before you apply**
 If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.
   

  

 Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.
   

  

 To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.
   

  

 If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Sportsbook & Gaming Analyst, NCE
matched_skills: SQL, Python, Data Modelling, Statistical Analysis, Data Visualization
gaps_in_skill: dbt, dbt, dbt, dbt, dbt, understanding of sportsbook and casino KPIs, A/B testing, cohort analysis, segmentation
description:
**Company Description**
 Sports betting, gaming and interactive entertainment is changing, and we’re leading that change. By putting people first. By placing exciting, engaging and entertaining experiences at more fingertips than ever before. We're pulling into pole position by pushing boundaries further. With innovation. With technology. But most importantly, with people like you. Because when you join Entain, it’s your game. So let’s win together.
   

  

**Job Description**
 The Sportsbook \& Casino Analyst provides actionable insights that improve product performance, optimise player engagement, and support commercial growth across the NCE region. The role focuses on understanding customer behaviour in sportsbook and casino, identifying trends and opportunities, and delivering clear recommendations that inform product strategy, marketing, and commercial decisions.
   

  

 By combining data exploration with robust analysis, the analyst ensures stakeholders can make confident, evidence\-based decisions to maximize both player experience and revenue outcomes. The role is responsible for uncovering opportunities, shaping initiatives, and equipping stakeholders with the insights and tools needed to enhance performance and ROI.
   

  

 By turning complex data into clear, actionable recommendations, the Sportsbook \& Gaming Analyst ensures that decision\-making across the region is insight\-led. The role also contributes to building the foundations of the NCE BI \& Analytics function, embedding best practice and supporting a culture of evidence\-based growth.
   

  

**Product \& Customer Insights**
* Analyse sportsbook and casino performance, identifying key drivers of revenue, margin, and player activity.
* Monitor player behaviour, wagering patterns, and product usage to highlight opportunities for growth and retention.
* Evaluate content relevance and effectiveness, new product launches, and feature adoption to assess impact and recommend improvements.
* Apply statistical techniques (cohort analysis, segmentation, A/B testing) to understand engagement, conversion, and churn.


**Data \& Reporting**
* Build and maintain datasets and KPIs that provide a reliable view of sportsbook and casino performance.
* Work hands\-on with SQL and data modelling tools (e.g. dbt) to transform raw data into trusted reporting and insights.
* Develop dashboards and reporting that provide visibility of key product and customer metrics across the region.
* Continuously improve reporting processes to ensure efficiency, accuracy, and alignment with commercial priorities.


**Collaboration \& Impact**
* Translate complex data into simple, actionable insights that help product, trading, and commercial teams make better decisions.
* Provide input into roadmap prioritisation by quantifying the commercial impact of product initiatives.
* Collaborate with compliance and safer gambling teams to ensure analysis supports regulatory and player protection objectives.
* Share best practices in analysis, reporting, and product measurement across the BI \& Analytics function.


**Qualifications**
 It is essential for this role to have strong, proven experience with Python and SQL\*
   

  

* Strong knowledge of SQL, with the ability to extract and analyse large datasets to support product and commercial analysis.
* Familiarity with Python, R, or similar tools for statistical analysis and modelling.
* Skilled in data visualization and storytelling, with experience using Power BI or similar tools to communicate insights.
* Understanding of sportsbook and casino KPIs, including turnover, margin, RTP, player value, and engagement metrics.
* Experience with A/B testing, cohort analysis, or segmentation to evaluate product and customer outcomes.
* Good understanding of data modelling and ETL concepts; exposure to dbt or GitHub is an advantage.
* Proficiency in Excel for quick analysis, modelling, and reporting.
* Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills,with a focus on simplifying complexity and highlighting what matters most.
* Clear communicator, able to translate complex analysis into simple insights for product, commercial, and leadership teams.
* Fluency in English


**Additional Information**
 At Entain, we know that signing top players requires a great starting package, and plenty of support to inspire peak performance. Join us, and a competitive salary is just the beginning. Working for us in London, you can expect to receive great benefits like:
   

  

* Generous group bonus scheme
* Hybrid working
* Private medical insurance
* Pension scheme – matched to 6%
* Ability to buy and sell holiday
* Free subscription to wellbeing app Unmind
* Additional “It’s Your Game” day off to use at either Christmas or New Year
* Entain \& Enhance days – 2 paid days off to focus on your professional or personal development
* Share save scheme


 And outside of this, you’ll have the chance to turn recognition from leaders and colleagues into amazing prizes. Join a winning team of talented people and be a part of an inclusive and supportive community where everyone is celebrated for being themselves.
   

  

*Should you need any adjustments or accommodations to the recruitment process, at either application or interview, please contact us.*
**Equal Opportunities.**
 If you need any reasonable adjustments at any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us and we'll support you.
   

  

 We're committed to creating a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected and able to be themselves.
   

  

 We're an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from everyone and we do not discriminate based on race, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion or belief, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital or civil partnership status, pregnancy or maternity, or any other status protected by law.
   

  

 We comply with all applicable recruitment regulations and employment laws in the jurisdictions where we operate, ensuring ethical and compliant hiring practices globally.

description:
**About The Role**
 We are Balfour Beatty, you may not know us by name but you definitely will have seen what we do. We are one of the UK’s largest construction companies building iconic buildings and structures that will last for generations to come.
   

  

 Are you keen to start your career with one of the UK’s biggest construction companies? If you're searching for a placement program that not only fosters your personal development but also offers a chance to collaborate with fellow placements, Balfour Beatty is the perfect employer for you.
   

  

 If you would like to leave your own legacy and say to your family and friends, ‘I helped build that’ then
 **\#BEBalfourBeatty** 
 and we can build the future together.
   

  

**BElong at Balfour Beatty and join our Early Career development programme** 
 .
   

  

 What you'll be doing
   

  

 As a Finance Summer Placement, you’ll support the finance team on a large, fast‑moving project and gain hands‑on experience with how finance supports real operational decisions. You’ll work with finance, commercial and operational teams to help ensure costs are understood, controlled and clearly reported.
   

  

 You’ll build practical finance skills, develop confidence working with data, and see how financial insight supports performance across a complex business environment.
   

  

**You’ll Be Involved In**
* Reviewing and checking accounting information to ensure accuracy
* Responding to day‑to‑day finance queries, including timesheets and expenses
* Tracking and monitoring project costs, highlighting risks or variances
* Supporting analysis and reporting to provide clear, data‑driven insight
* Working with operational and commercial teams to improve financial clarity
* Helping improve finance processes and reporting approaches
* Supporting the wider finance team during busy periods


 This placement offers real exposure to how finance works in practice and is an excellent opportunity to develop skills that will support a future career
   

  

 Who we're looking for
   

  

 Our Summer Placements are targeted at university students studying a Finance degree offering a programme during the summer break. This placement provides a valuable opportunity to gain hands\-on experience and build professional skills early in your academic journey.
   

  

 Why work for us
   

  

 Kickstart your career at Balfour Beatty, where you’ll join our Early Careers development programme and receive tailored support from day one. You’ll join a community of likeminded individuals whilst developing essential career and life skills.
   

  

 Alongside a great salary and benefits package, you'll gain invaluable experience and skills that pave the way for exciting opportunities and career progression. This is more than just a job—it's a pathway to a promising future.

description:
As a Finance Intern for Belmond Trains \& Cruises, you are part of a team that is responsible for maintaining financial accuracy. In this role, which has a duration of 15 months, you will manage credit card reconciliations, update transaction logs and prepare reports, while also supporting broader finance functions including exchange rate updates, refund processing and ledger postings. Based in London and reporting to the Trains \& Cruises Financial Controller, this is an exciting opportunity for a dynamic individual with a passion for excellence and storytelling in travel, a strong cultural awareness and an unswerving approach to maintaining standards. If you're looking to develop your skills and be part of a legacy of luxury, this is your moment.
   

  

**Primary Responsibilities Include:**
* Updating the BACS payment log for GBP, USA and EUR by accurately inputting receipts and reconciling them with bank statements.
* Routinely reconcile credit card statements to ensure accuracy of transactions and resolve any discrepancies promptly.
* Managing the processing of company expenses in a timely and efficient manner.
* Updating exchange rates in Travel Studio on a weekly basis to ensure financial activities reflect current market conditions.
* Support the team in reconciling balance sheets including intercompany holdings, intercompany payable/receivables, gift card redemption, bank reconciliation and deferred revenue.


**Requirements**
**What You Bring:**
* Previous experience in a finance or accounting role advantageous.
* Strong mathematical skills and good financial understanding.
* Ability to analyse financial data and provide insights.
* Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and willingness to learn and adapt to new challenges.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other financial software.


**Benefits**
**What We Offer:**
 At Belmond Trains and Cruises, we pride ourselves on fostering a culture built on kindness, active listening, and genuine connections with our colleagues and guests. We offer a comprehensive range of compensation, perks and benefits including:
   

  

* Health \& Dental insurance plans
* Discover Belmond Programme which includes complimentary and preferred rate experiences at our iconic destinations
* Group Income Protection \- Group Life Insurance \- Employer contribution pension
* Team social events
* Wellness programme throughout the year


 We strive to create an environment where our employees can thrive both personally and professionally. With a commitment to your ongoing development, you'll have the opportunity to learn and grow alongside some of the most talented individuals in the industry. Join us and be part of creating unforgettable moments for our guests, communities, and each
   

  

**This is your moment. Apply today!**
**About Us**
 Belmond Trains \& Cruises whisk guests away on evocative adventures across land and water. The legendary Venice Simplon\-Orient\-Express journeys between captivating European cities. Sister train British Pullman runs lavish day excursions around the UK from London. Royal Scotsman offers thrilling escapes in the Scottish Highlands. Eastern \& Oriental Express connects ancient wonders and cosmopolitan cities in Southeast Asia. On the water, Les Bateaux Belmond luxury barges cruise through Burgundy, Champagne and Provence.
   

  

 With our vast offerings, the opportunities to craft a unique career here are endless. Join us and achieve the truly exceptional.
   

  

**The Belmond \& LVMH Family**
 Belmond Trains \& Cruises is proud to be part of LVMH and the Belmond global collection of iconic hotels, trains and river cruises, all creating exceptional experiences worldwide. When you join one of our properties, you join a global family of history\-rich hotels and breathtaking destinations, where our employees are at the heart of the Belmond experience.

title: Analyst, Finance

gaps_in_skill: ACCA studies, Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A), Financial Reconciliation, Spend Management
description:
**Company Description**
 We’re Checkout.com – you might not know our name, but companies like eBay, ASOS, Klarna, Uber Eats, and Sony do. That moment when you check out online? We make it happen.
   

  

 Checkout.com is where the world checks out. Our global network powers billions of transactions every year, making money move without making a fuss. We spent years perfecting a service most people will never notice. Because when digital payments just work, businesses grow, customers stay, and no one stops to think about why.
   

  

 With 19 offices spanning six continents, we feel at home everywhere – but London is our HQ. Wherever our people work their magic, they’re fast\-moving, performance\-obsessed, and driven by being better every day. Ideal. Because a role here isn’t just another job; it’s a career\-defining opportunity to build the future of fintech.
   

  

**Job Details**
 As part of Checkout.com’s \#NextGen Early Careers Program you’ll work alongside experienced professionals, gaining hands\-on experience and a comprehensive introduction to digital payments and technology. The program offers an extensive learning experience through technical training, professional development workshops, and direct access to industry experts. You'll be part of a global Early Careers community, with regular networking events, mentoring opportunities, and social activities designed to build connections and accelerate your growth and leadership potential. By the end of the scheme, you’ll be equipped with the knowledge, experience, and confidence to make a lasting impact at Checkout.com and beyond.
   

  

 Checkout.com is looking for an ambitious
 **Finance Analyst Apprentice** 
 to join our Finance team. This is a unique opportunity to kickstart your career in Fintech while gaining a world\-class professional qualification. You will report directly to the Regional Finance Director and work within a global team that is building a best\-in\-class financial environment.
   

  

 As an apprentice, you will spend 20% of your time dedicated to your
 **ACCA (Association of Chartered Certified Accountants)** 
 studies, with the remaining 80% spent gaining hands\-on experience in our fast\-paced EU Finance department.
   

  

**How You’ll Make An Impact**
 The following is from a Financial Control perspective:
   

  

* Month\-End Support: Assist with monthly postings in our accounting system, learning the heartbeat of a global business's financial cycle.
* Financial Accuracy: Help perform reconciliations, prepare journal entries, and assist in variance analysis to ensure our numbers are precise.
* Spend Management: Review supplier invoices to ensure correct account coding and cost center allocation, gaining insight into how a global company manages its expenses.
* Process Evolution: Contribute to the continuous improvement of our month\-end processes as we scale our operations across Europe.
* Cross\-Team Collaboration: Act as a point of support for our Accounts Payable and FP\&A (Financial Planning \& Analysis) teams, helping resolve queries and share data.


 In addition to the above, the programme would include exposure to a variety of teams within the Finance organisation
   

  

**Eligibility Requirements**
 This is an entry level role. You must qualify for Level 7 Apprenticeship funding at the date this role is due to commence in Oct 2026\. Further information about the Apprenticeship funding can be found here.
   

  

* Academic Qualifications: You will have either:
+ A degree qualification
- OR

+ You have completed a Level 4 AAT qualification before the September start date
+ \+ 2 A Levels (or equivalent) and 3 GCSEs including a pass in English and Maths

* Visa sponsorship: We are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role.


**What We’re Looking For**
* Academic Ambition: You are eager to begin your journey toward becoming a Chartered Accountant via the ACCA pathway.
* Analytical Mindset: You have a natural comfort with numbers and a high attention to detail.
* Resilience: The ability to work toward tight deadlines in a fast\-paced, high\-growth work environment.
* Tech Savvy: Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets. Any prior exposure to accounting systems (like Workday) is a huge plus, but not a dealbreaker—we will train you!
* Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills and a "team player" attitude.


**Additional Information**
**Bring all of you to work**
 We create the conditions for high performers to thrive, through real ownership, fewer blockers, and work that makes a difference from day one.
   

  

 Here, you’ll move fast, take on meaningful challenges, and be recognized for the impact you deliver. It’s a place where ambition gets met with opportunity, and where your growth is in your hands.
   

  

 We work as one team, and we back each other to succeed. So whatever your background or identity, if you’re ready to grow and make a difference, you’ll be right at home here.
   

  

 It’s important we set you up for success and make our process as accessible as possible. So let us know in your application, or tell your recruiter directly, if you need anything to make your experience or working environment more comfortable.
   

  

**Life at** 
**Checkout.com**
 We understand that work is just one part of your life. Our hybrid working model offers flexibility, with three days per week in the office to support collaboration and connection.

description:
**Job title** 
 : Market Risk Specialist
 



**Pay** 
 : Up to £600 Per Day (Inside IR35, PAYE)
 



**Hours** 
 : Part\-time (21 hours minimum) or full\-time
 



**Duration** 
 : 12 months
 



**Location** 
 : 2 days per week in the Stratford, London office (50% in office from 1 September)
 



  





 Your recruitment contact is Olivia Greig via Olivia.Greig@hays.com.
 



  





**About the organisation** 




 Our client is an independent regulatory body in the UK responsible for ensuring the financial markets work well for the consumers and the economy, promoting market integrity and effective competition.
 



  





**About the role** 




 We are seeking a contract Market Risk Specialist to support the development of policy proposals as part of the review of the market risk capital requirements for specialised investment firms. This work forms part of a broader programme of reform signalled by the organisations Chief Executive in October 2024 and subsequently reflected in the UK Government’s Financial Services Growth and Competitiveness Strategy published in July 2025\.
 



  





 The successful candidate will provide specialist technical support to the team as it develops proposals for consultation, with a Consultation Paper (CP) scheduled for publication in October 2026 and supports the subsequent refinement and finalisation of policy proposals following consultation.
 



  





**Role Responsibilities:** 



* Provide technical market risk expertise to support the review of capital requirements for specialised investment firms.
* Support the development of policy proposals for inclusion in the Consultation Paper scheduled for publication in October 2026\.
* Contribute to policy analysis, drafting and internal challenge on complex market risk issues.
* Assist in assessing the prudential and wider regulatory implications of potential policy options.
* Support the team in reviewing, analysing and responding to stakeholder feedback received through the consultation process.
* Help ensure policy proposals are evidence\-based, coherent and deliverable within the required timetable.
* Work closely with existing team members and relevant internal stakeholders to maintain momentum on this high\-profile and technically complex project.



  





**Skills** 
 :
 



  





**Minimum** 
 :
 


* Strong technical knowledge of market risk and prudential capital requirements.
* Experience of analysing complex regulatory or policy issues, ideally in financial services regulation, risk, prudential policy or a related field.
* Ability to apply technical expertise to support the development of policy proposals.
* Strong drafting skills, including the ability to contribute to consultation and policy documents.



  





**Essential** 
 :
 


* Ability to interpret and assess stakeholder feedback and translate this into policy recommendations.
* Strong analytical skills and sound judgement.
* Ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment and deliver to demanding timelines.



  





**Disability Confident: our hiring approach** 








 We’re proud to be a Disability Confident Employer, and therefore, people or individuals with disabilities and long\-term conditions who best meet the minimum criteria for a role will go through to the next stage of the recruitment process. In cases of high application volumes, we may progress applicants whose experience most closely matches the role’s key requirements.
 



  





**What are the next steps?** 




 If you are interested in the role, click the apply button and we will get in touch with you to discuss next steps.

In our flat and agile organization, you will get all the opportunities to make real impacts and deliver change. Most importantly, while you achieve greatness, we will ensure you have an amazing experience at WeLab!

**About You**
* You're a go\-getter with mad juggling skills (or multiple hats) who can thrive in a fast\-paced, agile environment
* You enjoy doing purpose\-led and meaningful work

 :
   

  

* Provide end\-to\-end Premium Finance loan administration, including handling new Premium Finance applications, document verification, follow\-up with customers/agents, and ensuring smooth drawdown.

* Provide administrative support for loan drawdown, repayment schedule creation, customer follow\-ups, and policy status monitoring (for Premium Finance accounts).


**What Is Required And What We’re Looking For**
* Form 5 graduate or above
* Minimum 1 year of experience in telemarketing, customer service, loan administration, or related areas
* Mandatory: Hands\-on experience in Premium Finance, including processing Premium Finance applications
* Proactive, customer\-oriented, with a clear and professional telephone manner
* Fluent spoken Cantonese, with basic English and Mandarin
* Basic computer literacy, including Chinese and English input
* Willing to work shifts, including Saturdays


**Perks And Benefits To Enrich Your Experience At WeLab**

We allow flexible time offs to take care of personal matters and to enjoy time with family and friends.

* Work smart, play hard – Connect with your colleagues over food and drinks at the stocked pantry, in the spacious social area with board games and a ping pong table, or over activities like hiking, yoga, badminton, etc.
* A big and contagious smile on everyone’s face to make you happier :)

description:
**Position Description** 




 We are seeking an experienced Sustainability Analyst to join the Energy Transition \& Sustainability Research Team. Reporting to the Head of Energy Transition \& Sustainability Research, you will be required to drive differentiated, investment‑relevant research at the intersection of energy transition, AI transformation and capital markets.
 



 The successful candidate will spearhead our team’s Sustainability Research effort and drive ideas generation on sustainability topics that interlinks with sector teams within Research Department. This role is suited for experienced individuals who possess strong financial modelling skills, as well as a deep understanding of market dynamics in energy transition and AI transformation.
 



  





**Key Areas of Responsibilities** 



* Develop thematic research on energy transition topics e.g. net‑zero pathways, carbon costs, energy security, policy impact, technology adoption curves
* Assess how AI adoption reshapes power/energy demand, emissions intensity and competitive positioning across multiple sectors
* Familiar with sustainability challenges linked to AI deployment e.g. power intensity, data‑center infrastructure, regulatory \& social considerations
* Analyse how governance factors e.g. capital market reforms, value\-up program could reshape equity markets and drive valuation upside/downside
* Produce high‑conviction investment ideas that is supported by rigorous financial analysis and valuation frameworks
* Able to link sustainability factors to earnings/cashflows/valuation, quantify the financial impact and make investment recommendations
* Work closely with regional and sector analysts to integrate sustainability themes into broader equity research
* Maintain and enhance the Sustainability Research franchise in the investment community



  





**Requirements** 



* Post graduate or above, with a minimum of 6 to 8 years’ experience from buyside or sellside
* CPA/CFA qualification would be an advantage
* Prior experience in sector coverage of energy / utilities / industrials / materials are welcomed
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience addressing institutional investors
* Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and meet demanding publication timelines
* Self\-driven and collaborative mindset

description:

Nasdaq\-listed since 2019 with a market cap of around $22\.7 billion (as of late January 2026\), Futu is highly stable and compliant, holding over 100 global licenses \- including SFC approval for virtual assets in Hong Kong and FINRA in the US. It serves over 28\.2 million users across 200\+ countries/regions, with strong growth: Q3 2025 revenues jumped 86% YoY to HK$6\.4B (US$823M), client assets reached HK$1\.24T (US$159B, \+79% YoY), and funded accounts grew :43% YoY to 3\.13 million. High user retention (\>98%) and diversified revenue (brokerage, margin financing, wealth management) drive consistent profitability.
   





 In Web3, Futu is rapidly bridging TradFi and crypto: compliant spot trading launched in Hong Kong (2024, $0 commissions on BTC/ETH/etc.), tokenized funds/RWAs through partnerships (e.g., ChinaAMC), and crypto ETF access. Crypto activity exploded in 2025 (e.g., assets \+90% QoQ, trading volume \+161% QoQ), with heavy focus on secure, regulated features. To fuel this growth, Futu is actively hiring Web3 talent\- like product managers, developers, and compliance roles\-building an innovative, compliant edge in digital assets.
   





**Role Overview** 




 Responsible for designing, optimizing, and driving the end\-to\-end lifecycle management of exchange derivative product lines (such as perpetual futures, options, structured products, etc.). Deeply understand market demands, regulatory trends, and technical implementation, integrating quantitative strategies and risk management systems to build a competitive derivatives trading platform, assisting users in achieving risk hedging, arbitrage, and leveraged trading objectives.

* Lead the product design of cryptocurrency derivatives (futures, options, perpetual swaps, etc.), including contract specifications, funding rates, settlement mechanisms, and margin models
* Coordinate with technical teams on product development requirements and assist marketing and operations teams in formulating derivatives promotion plans



**(Focus: user journeys and market entry.)** 



* Perform Web3 industry/competitor analysis (Binance, Bybit, Deribit, DeFi) and produce reports.
* Design front\-end transaction experiences (flows, disclosures, hedging/arbitrage features) aligned with research.
* Manage full requirement\-to\-launch process for user\-facing products.
* Develop strategies from user behavior, trends, and tokenized/RWA innovations.
* Collaborate with marketing/ops/tech for GTM and promotions


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree or higher in Finance, Economics, Computer Science, or related fields preferred. overseas study preferred.
* Bachelor's or above; overseas study preferred.
* Fresh graduate or 1\-3 years in Web3 industry.
* Basic front\-end transaction understanding; strong Web3 trend sense and C\-end design experience.
* Solid market/user research and analytical skills.
* Fluent English (speaking/listening/reading/writing)
